anchor.snippet: is a quadrilateral that has at least one pair of parallel sides

annotations.label: Bases and legs of a trapezoid

anchor.snippet: The parallel sides are called the bases of the trapezoid
Under an exclusive definition a trapezoid is a quadrilateral having exactly one pair of parallel sides
Under an inclusive definition, a trapezoid is any quadrilateral with at least one pair of parallel sides
A trapezoid can also be defined as any quadrilateral that is not a parallelogram
some sources use the term proper trapezoid to describe trapezoids with exactly one pair of parallel sides
An isosceles trapezoid is a trapezoid where the base angles have the same measure

annotations.label: Isosceles trapezoid has equal legs and reflection symmetry

anchor.snippet: the two legs are also of equal length and it has reflection symmetry
An acute trapezoid is a trapezoid with two adjacent acute angles on its longer base
a three-sided trapezoid, meaning it is a trapezoid wherein two trapezoid's legs have equal lengths as the trapezoid's base at the top

annotations.label: Isosceles trapezoid is convex hull of antiparallelogram

anchor.snippet: The isosceles trapezoid is the convex hull of an antiparallelogram
An obtuse trapezoid, on the other hand, has one acute and one obtuse angle on each base
A right trapezoid is a trapezoid with two adjacent right angles
which was used by James Garfield to prove the Pythagorean theorem
A tangential trapezoid is a trapezoid that has an incircle

annotations.label: Condition of existence of a trapezoid

anchor.snippet: can constitute the consecutive sides of a non-parallelogram trapezoid with a and b parallel only when
the following properties are equivalent, and each implies that the quadrilateral is a trapezoid

annotations.label: Midpoints of opposite sides and diagonal intersection collinear

anchor.snippet: The midpoints of two opposite sides of the trapezoid and the intersection of the diagonals are collinear

annotations.label: Cosines of adjacent angles sum to zero

anchor.snippet: The cosines of two adjacent angles sum to 0, as do the cosines of the other two angles

annotations.label: Cotangents of adjacent angles sum to zero

anchor.snippet: The cotangents of two adjacent angles sum to 0, as do the cotangents of the other two adjacent angles

annotations.label: Bimedian divides trapezoid into equal areas

anchor.snippet: One bimedian divides the quadrilateral into two quadrilaterals of equal areas

annotations.label: Bimedian length equals sum of other sides

anchor.snippet: Twice the length of the bimedian connecting the midpoints of two opposite sides equals the sum of the lengths of the other sides

annotations.label: Further equivalent conditions for parallel sides

anchor.snippet: the following properties are equivalent, and each implies that opposite sides a and b are parallel
The midsegment or median of a trapezoid is the segment that joins the midpoints of the legs
Its length m is equal to the average of the lengths of the bases a and b of the trapezoid
The height (or altitude) is the perpendicular distance between the bases
the height of a trapezoid h can be determined by the length of its four sides using the formula
of a trapezoid is given by the product of the midsegment (the average of the two bases) and the height
Bhāskara I derived the following formula for the area of a trapezoid with consecutive sides
this formula reduces to Heron's formula for the area of a triangle
Another equivalent formula for the area, which more closely resembles Heron's formula
The bimedian connecting the parallel sides bisects the area

annotations.label: Triangle to midpoint of leg bisects area

anchor.snippet: Any triangle connecting the two ends of one leg to the midpoint of the other leg is also half of the area

annotations.label: Harmonic mean of bases through diagonal intersection

anchor.snippet: is the harmonic mean of the lengths of the bases

annotations.label: Line through diagonal and extended side intersections bisects bases

anchor.snippet: The line that goes through both the intersection point of the extended nonparallel sides and the intersection point of the diagonals, bisects each base

annotations.label: Center of area of a trapezoid

anchor.snippet: The center of area ( center of mass for a uniform lamina ) lies along the line segment joining the midpoints of the parallel sides

annotations.label: Ratio in which center of area divides segment

anchor.snippet: The center of area divides this segment in the ratio
If the angle bisectors to angles A and B intersect at P , and the angle bisectors to angles C and D intersect at Q
approximating the region under the graph of the function by a collection of trapezoids, so this method is called the trapezoidal rule
When any rectangle is viewed in perspective from a position which is centered on one axis but not the other, it appears to be an isosceles trapezoid
A spherical or hyperbolic trapezoid is a quadrilateral with two opposite sides, the legs, each of whose two adjacent angles sum to the same quantity
each of the resulting two pieces is an isosceles trapezoid with two right angles, called a Saccheri quadrilateral
each of the resulting four pieces is a quadrilateral with three right angles called a Lambert quadrilateral
A trapezoidal number is a set of positive integers obtained by summing consecutively two or more positive integers greater than one
A crossed ladders problem is the problem of finding the distance between the parallel sides of a right trapezoid
